varieties.name: Bambusa textilis 'Gracilis' (Slender Weaver Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 6-10m, culms 2.5-4cm diameter, graceful upright habit, smooth green culms, tight clumping growth, fine-textured feathery leaves
varieties.yield_notes: Fast-growing, reaches 3-4m in first 12 months from 200mm container
varieties.availability: Bamboo Land (Hervey Bay), Living Bamboo (humid subtropical climates/Samford Valley), Aussie Bamboo (Sydney), Zen Bamboo nurseries, widely available throughout Queensland and NSW

varieties.name: Bambusa multiplex 'Alphonse Karr'
varieties.traits: Height 6-8m, culms 1.5-2.5cm diameter, bright golden-yellow culms with random green stripes, new shoots with pinkish hue, dense bushy growth
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate to fast growth, dense clumping
varieties.availability: Living Bamboo (humid subtropical climates), Bamboo Land (Hervey Bay), major Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Bambusa multiplex 'Goldstripe'
varieties.traits: Height 3-4m, culms 1-1.5cm diameter, green culms with cream striping, compact upright growth, dark green foliage
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate growth, tight clumping to 1m diameter
varieties.availability: Living Bamboo (humid subtropical climates), Zen Bamboo (Canberra/Newcastle), Bamboo Land (Hervey Bay)

varieties.name: Bambusa multiplex 'Chinese Dwarf'
varieties.traits: Height 2.5-4m (can reach 6-8m if untrimmed), culms pencil-thin, incredibly lush and bushy, large dark green foliage, tropical appearance
varieties.yield_notes: Fast and dense growth when trimmed regularly
varieties.availability: Bamboo Land (Hervey Bay), Zen Bamboo, Queensland bamboo suppliers

varieties.name: Dendrocalamus minor var. amoenus (Ghost Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 8-12m, culms 4-8cm diameter, pale blue-green to silvery culms with white waxy bloom when young, striking ghostly appearance

varieties.name: Dendrocalamus giganteus (Giant Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height up to 20m in subtropical humid subtropical climates (30m+ in tropics), culms 20-30cm diameter, dull green to dark blue-green with white waxy crust when young, second-tallest bamboo species globally
varieties.yield_notes: Can grow 30cm per day under optimal conditions, produces enormous biomass
varieties.availability: Bamboo Land (Hervey Bay - seedlings from 2010 flowering), specialist tropical bamboo suppliers

varieties.name: Bambusa lako (Timor Black Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 12-16m, culms 8-12cm diameter, glossy black culms with occasional green striping, upright growth habit, medium-sized clumping
varieties.availability: Bamboo South Coast, specialist Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Gigantochloa atroviolacea (Javanese Black/Java Black)
varieties.traits: Height 12-16m, culms 8-12cm diameter, dark purple-black culms, can look untidy after frosty winters
varieties.availability: Specialist bamboo nurseries in Queensland

varieties.name: Bambusa arnhemica (Northern Australian Native Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height up to 12m, culms up to 12cm diameter, robust clumping growth, thorny, large culms, native to Arnhem Land
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate growth, adapted to Australian conditions
varieties.availability: Specialist native plant nurseries, limited availability

varieties.name: Bambusa eutuldoides 'China Gold'
varieties.traits: Height 8-12m, wonderful yellow culms, striking ornamental appearance
varieties.availability: Bamboo nurseries in humid subtropical climates and Queensland

varieties.name: Bambusa oldhamii (Giant Timber Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 12-18m, culms 8-15cm diameter, large clumping bamboo, thick-walled culms, erect growth
varieties.availability: Widely available from Queensland bamboo nurseries

varieties.name: Bambusa ventricosa (Buddha's Belly Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 3-5m (dwarf form) to 15m (normal form), distinctive swollen internodes when stressed or container-grown, unique sculptural appearance

varieties.name: Bambusa dolichomerithalla 'Silverstripe'
varieties.traits: Height 8-12m, culms with silver-white striping on green background, elegant appearance, upright growth
varieties.availability: Living Bamboo (humid subtropical climates), specialist nurseries

varieties.name: Bambusa tuldoides 'Ventricosa'
varieties.traits: Height 8-12m, slightly swollen internodes, upright growth, medium-sized clumping
varieties.availability: Queensland bamboo suppliers

varieties.name: Bambusa bambos (Giant Thorny Bamboo)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to tropical monsoon climates and Southern India
varieties.traits: Height 20-30m (up to 40m recorded), culms 10-18cm diameter, dark green culms, thorny branches, most massive Bambusa species, thick-walled culms
varieties.yield_notes: High biomass production, occupies 15% of India's bamboo forests
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout tropical monsoon climates, tropical monsoon climates State Bamboo Corporation, local nurseries, home garden propagation common

varieties.name: Dendrocalamus strictus (Solid Bamboo, Male Bamboo)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to tropical monsoon climates and throughout India
varieties.traits: Height 8-16m, culms 2.5-8cm diameter, dull green becoming brown with maturity, culms often solid especially in dry conditions, straight growth with slight zig-zag toward top
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate growth, occupies 53% of India's total bamboo area
varieties.availability: KFRI Peechi, JNTBGRI Thiruvananthapuram, Uravu Wayanad, tropical monsoon climates Forest Department nurseries, tropical monsoon climates State Bamboo Mission outlets

varieties.name: Ochlandra travancorica (Kerala Reed Bamboo, Travancore Reed)
varieties.origin: Endemic to Western Ghats, tropical monsoon climates - found nowhere else naturally
varieties.traits: Height 2-6m, culms 2.5-5cm diameter, thin-walled reed bamboo, small graceful species, forms extensive brakes on hilltops and along streams
varieties.yield_notes: Forms large monospecific patches, moderate growth rate
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Forest Department, tropical monsoon climates State Bamboo Corporation, KFRI Peechi, JNTBGRI, limited due to conservation concerns

varieties.name: Bambusa tulda (Bengal Bamboo, Indian Timber Bamboo)
varieties.origin: Indian subcontinent - Bengal, Assam, Northeast India
varieties.traits: Height 6-23m, culms 5-10cm diameter, near-solid culms, greyish-green when mature, yellowish-green shoots with powdery top, incredible tensile strength (up to 60,000 psi)
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely fast-growing - shoots can grow 70cm per day during rainy season, completes height growth in 2-3 months
varieties.availability: Indian forestry departments, bamboo nurseries, tissue culture facilities

varieties.name: Dendrocalamus giganteus (Giant Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 20-30m in tropical monsoon climates (up to 42m in optimal tropical conditions), culms 20-30cm diameter, second-tallest bamboo globally, dull green to dark blue-green culms with white waxy crust when young
varieties.yield_notes: Can grow up to 46cm in 24 hours under optimal conditions, produces 20-30 tonnes/hectare annually - 2.7 times more than D. latiflorus
varieties.availability: National Bamboo Mission nurseries, Uravu (Wayanad), tropical monsoon climates agricultural extension centers, progressive farmer networks

varieties.name: Dendrocalamus brandisii (Brandis's Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 15-25m, culms 10-15cm diameter, light green culms, magnificent ornamental with large leaves, rapid growth (can exceed 2 feet/day)
varieties.yield_notes: Very fast growth, high biomass production

varieties.name: Dendrocalamus asper (Giant Rough Bamboo, Betung)
varieties.traits: Height 20-30m, culms 8-20cm diameter, fast-growing tropical clumping species (10cm+ per day), green culms, large-leafed
varieties.yield_notes: Extremely fast growth, adds 10cm or more per day
varieties.availability: National Bamboo Mission nurseries, tropical monsoon climates bamboo growers

varieties.name: Bambusa balcooa (Female Bamboo, Bhaluka)
varieties.origin: Northeast India - Assam, Bengal, traditional bamboo
varieties.traits: Height up to 22m (73 feet), culms 10-15cm diameter, tall stature, thick-walled culms, erect growth
varieties.availability: tropical monsoon climates Forest Research Institute, National Bamboo Mission outlets

varieties.name: Bambusa vulgaris (Common Bamboo)
varieties.origin: Native to Bangladesh, India, Sri Lanka, Southeast Asia, Southern China
varieties.traits: Height 10-20m, culms 4-10cm diameter, widely cultivated, naturalized globally, fast-growing, variable appearance
varieties.availability: Widely available throughout tropical monsoon climates, common in home gardens

varieties.name: Bambusa vulgaris 'Vittata' (Painted Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 10-15m, culms with striking yellow and green vertical stripes, ornamental variety, bright coloration

varieties.name: Thyrsostachys oliveri
varieties.traits: Short rhizomes, clumping habit, medium height
varieties.availability: Limited, research institutes

varieties.name: Dendrocalamus stocksii
varieties.origin: Western Ghats - Maharashtra (Sindhudurg), indigenous to region
varieties.traits: Grows abundantly in parts of Western Ghats, medium to large bamboo
varieties.yield_notes: Good productivity in Western Ghats climate
varieties.availability: Western Ghats conservation programs, research institutes

varieties.name: Melocanna baccifera (Berry Bamboo, Muli Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 10-20m, culms 5-10cm diameter, distinctive berry-like fleshy fruits, forms bamboo brakes
varieties.availability: Specialized collections in tropical monsoon climates, research institutes

varieties.name: Bambusa nutans (Mountain Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Medium-height bamboo, clumping habit, nodding culm tops
varieties.availability: Limited, specialty nurseries

varieties.name: Schizostachyum brachycladum (Golden Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Ornamental species with beautiful golden coloration, graceful appearance

varieties.name: Bambusa textilis 'Gracilis'
varieties.traits: Height 6-10m, graceful upright habit, smooth green culms
varieties.availability: Some tropical monsoon climates nurseries, better suited to cooler regions

varieties.name: Guadua angustifolia (Guadua Bamboo)
varieties.traits: Height 18-30m, culms 10-20cm diameter, fastest-growing commercially used bamboo species, exceptional structural properties
varieties.availability: Limited, specialist collections only, not yet commercially cultivated in India

varieties.name: Ochlandra wightii
varieties.origin: Endemic to Western Ghats, tropical monsoon climates
varieties.traits: Reed bamboo, similar to O. travancorica, supports endemic rodent species Platacanthomys lasyrus
varieties.availability: Conservation programs, limited availability

varieties.name: Ochlandra scriptoria
varieties.origin: Western Ghats, tropical monsoon climates endemic
varieties.traits: Reed bamboo species, part of Tropical monsoon endemic Ochlandra genus
varieties.availability: KFRI Peechi, conservation programs

varieties.name: Ochlandra beddomei
varieties.traits: Reed bamboo, one of Tropical monsoon endemic Ochlandra species
varieties.availability: Research collections only

varieties.name: Ochlandra setigera
varieties.traits: Reed bamboo species endemic to Tropical monsoon Western Ghats
varieties.availability: Research collections

varieties.name: Ochlandra ebracteata
varieties.traits: Reed bamboo endemic to tropical monsoon climates region
varieties.availability: Research collections

varieties.name: Ochlandra sivagiriana
varieties.traits: Reed bamboo species from Western Ghats
varieties.availability: Research programs
